title: Reuters: AI-referred shoppers browse longer and spend more per visit
friendlyTitle: AI recommendations are sending higher-value shoppers to stores
summary: Reuters reported Adobe Analytics data showing U.S. retail traffic referred by LLMs such as ChatGPT and Gemini rose 138% year over year in May. Adobe said AI-referred shoppers generated 53% more revenue per visit, converted 54% more often, and spent 53% more time on retail sites than non-AI referrals.
friendlySummary: Adobe data reported by Reuters says shoppers coming from AI tools spend more time and money on retail sites. AI referral traffic also grew sharply from last year.
whyItMatters: AI assistants are becoming a commerce discovery channel. Retailers and small businesses should make product pages accurate, structured, and easy for AI systems to understand, because recommendations may increasingly start inside chat tools.
friendlyWhyItMatters: For businesses, being understandable to AI assistants may become as important as SEO. Clear product data, pricing, and useful pages matter more when shoppers start with ChatGPT or Gemini.
